subject: History
q: Bhavabhuti, Hastimalla and Kshemeshvara were famous:
sol: Ancient Sanskrit playwrights.

subject: History
q: Which ancient town is known for its water management?
sol: Dholavira has sophisticated stone-cut reservoirs.

subject: History
q: The Rakhmabai case of 1884 revolved around:
sol: Pivotal for the Age of Consent Act, 1891.

subject: Polity
q: Money Bill is defined in which Article?
sol: Article 110 defines Money Bills.

subject: Polity
q: Procedure established by Law is borrowed from:
sol: Taken from the Japanese Constitution.

subject: History
q: Kulyavapa and Dronavapa denote:
sol: Gupta era land measurement units.

subject: Environment
q: Eco-Sensitive Zones are defined under:
sol: Environment Protection Act, 1986.

subject: Polity
q: Right to Property was removed by which Amendment?
sol: 44th Amendment, 1978.

subject: Geography
q: Which river does not flow into the Arabian Sea?
sol: Mahanadi flows into the Bay of Bengal.

subject: Polity
q: Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee?
sol: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

subject: Geography
q: The 'Golden Quadrilateral' connects:
sol: Connects Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata.

subject: Economy
q: The term 'Capital Adequacy Ratio' is related to:
sol: Related to bank stability (Basel III).

subject: Geography
q: Which planet is known as the 'Earth's Twin'?
sol: Venus, due to size and mass similarity.

subject: History
q: The 'Vande Mataram' was first sung at INC session in:
sol: Calcutta session, 1896.

subject: History
q: Who wrote 'Hind Swaraj'?
sol: M.K. Gandhi in 1909.

subject: Geography
q: Equator does not pass through which country?
sol: Ethiopia lies north of the Equator.

subject: Polity
q: The 'Ninth Schedule' was added by which Amendment?
sol: 1st Amendment, 1951.

subject: Geography
q: Which layer of Atmosphere contains Ozone?
sol: Ozone layer is in the Stratosphere.

subject: Economy
q: GNP = GDP + ?
sol: Net Factor Income from Abroad.

subject: Polity
q: First Lok Sabha Speaker was:
sol: G.V. Mavalankar.

subject: Polity
q: Which Article of the Constitution provides for the 'Right to Education'?
sol: Added by the 86th Amendment Act, 2002.

subject: Geography
q: The 'Red Sea' is a strip of water between which two continents?
sol: It separates the Arabian Peninsula from northeastern Africa.

subject: Polity
q: Which committee recommended the 'Panchayati Raj' in India?
sol: The 1957 committee suggested a 3-tier structure.

subject: Geography
q: The 'Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve' is located in which states?
sol: It spans across Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Karnataka.

subject: Polity
q: Which Schedule of the Constitution deals with Anti-Defection?
sol: Added by the 52nd Amendment Act, 1985.

subject: History
q: The 'Cripps Mission' visited India in which year?
sol: Headed by Sir Stafford Cripps during WWII.

subject: Geography
q: Which port is known as the 'Jewel of all Ports' in India?
sol: It is a natural land-locked harbour.

subject: Polity
q: Who has the power to decide a 'Money Bill' in the State?
sol: The Speaker of the Legislative Assembly makes the final call.

subject: Economy
q: The term 'Stagflation' refers to:
sol: A combination of stagnant growth and high price rises.

subject: Geography
q: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?
sol: The Ural Mountains form the natural border.

subject: Polity
q: The 'Objective Resolution' was moved in the Constituent Assembly by:
sol: Moved on Dec 13, 1946; became the Preamble.

subject: Environment
q: The 'Bandipur National Park' is famous for:
sol: Part of Project Tiger, located in Karnataka.

subject: Polity
q: Which Article allows the Supreme Court to issue 'Writs'?
sol: Art 32 is for SC; Art 226 is for High Courts.

subject: History
q: The 'Regulating Act of 1773' established a Supreme Court at:
sol: Established at Fort William, Calcutta.

subject: Geography
q: Which current flows along the coast of Peru?
sol: Also known as the Peru Current; cold water current.

subject: Polity
q: The 'Joint Sitting' of Parliament is presided over by:
sol: Under Art 108, the Speaker presides.

subject: Economy
q: In India, 'Inflation' is primarily measured by:
sol: RBI now uses CPI (Combined) as the main anchor.

subject: Geography
q: Which layer of the Earth is called 'Sial'?
sol: Composed of Silica and Aluminium.

subject: History
q: Who was the first Satyagrahi in 'Individual Satyagraha'?
sol: Started by Gandhi in 1940.

subject: Environment
q: The 'Kyoto Protocol' is related to:
sol: International treaty to reduce CO2 emissions.

subject: History
q: The 'Swadeshi Movement' was officially proclaimed in response to:
sol: Proclaimed in 1905 at Calcutta Town Hall to protest Bengal's partition.

subject: Environment
q: Which gas is most responsible for 'Global Warming'?
sol: CO2 is the primary greenhouse gas emitted through human activities.

subject: History
q: The 'Quit India Movement' started after the failure of:
sol: Launched in Aug 1942 after the Cripps Mission failed to offer self-rule.

subject: Science
q: Which part of the brain controls body temperature and hunger?
sol: Hypothalamus acts as the body's smart control coordinating center.

subject: History
q: Who was the first Governor-General of Free India?
sol: Mountbatten served until June 1948; C. Rajagopalachari was the first Indian one.

subject: History
q: The 'National Song' of India was taken from which novel?
sol: Written by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay in 1882.

subject: Science
q: Which vitamin is synthesized in the human body by Sunlight?
sol: Skin produces Vitamin D when exposed to UV-B rays.

subject: History
q: The 'Simon Commission' was boycotted because:
sol: All 7 members were British, leading to 'Simon Go Back' slogans.

subject: Science
q: The 'Scurvy' disease is caused by the deficiency of:
sol: Lack of ascorbic acid (Vitamin C) leads to bleeding gums.

subject: History
q: Who founded the 'All India Muslim League' in 1906?
sol: Founded in Dacca to safeguard Muslim political rights.

subject: Science
q: Which metal is the best conductor of electricity?
sol: Silver has the highest electrical conductivity of all metals.

subject: History
q: The 'Lucknow Pact' of 1916 was signed between:
sol: It was a joint platform for self-government demands.

subject: Science
q: What is the unit of 'Power' in physics?
sol: One Watt is defined as 1 Joule per second.

subject: History
q: Who is known as the 'Frontier Gandhi'?
sol: Leader of the Khudai Khidmatgar movement.

subject: Science
q: Which fuel is used in Nuclear Reactors?
sol: Uranium-235 and Thorium-232 are both used in various stages.

subject: History
q: The 'Ghadar Party' was founded in which city?
sol: Founded in 1913 by Lala Hardayal and Sohan Singh Bhakna.

subject: Science
q: Which blood group is known as the 'Universal Donor'?
sol: O-negative blood can be given to patients of any blood type.

subject: History
q: The 'Rowlatt Act' (1919) was known as the 'Black Act' because:
sol: Allowed the British to imprison anyone for 2 years without a trial.

subject: Science
q: Which is the largest gland in the human body?
sol: The liver is the largest internal organ and gland.

subject: History
q: Who gave the slogan 'Do or Die' during the freedom struggle?
sol: Given during the launch of the Quit India Movement in 1942.

subject: Environment
q: Which of the following is the largest source of 'Marine Pollution'?
sol: Land-based sources account for the majority of ocean pollution.

subject: Economy
q: The 'Phillips Curve' describes the relationship between:
sol: Suggests an inverse relationship between inflation and unemployment.

subject: Geography
q: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
sol: Gujarat has a coastline of about 1,600 km.

subject: Environment
q: The 'Montreal Protocol' (1987) is related to:
sol: Agreement to phase out ozone-depleting substances.

subject: Economy
q: Who is the 'Lender of Last Resort' in the Indian economy?
sol: The RBI provides emergency liquidity to banks.

subject: Geography
q: The 'Silent Valley National Park' is located in:
sol: Located in the Palakkad district, Kerala.

subject: Polity
q: In which year was the 'Planning Commission' replaced by 'NITI Aayog'?
sol: NITI Aayog was established on January 1, 2015.

subject: Geography
q: Which pass connects Srinagar to Leh?
sol: Zoji La is a high mountain pass on NH 1.

subject: Economy
q: The 'Repo Rate' is the rate at which:
sol: Rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.

subject: Geography
q: The 'Great Barrier Reef' is located off the coast of:
sol: World's largest coral reef system, in Australia.

subject: Economy
q: Which of the following is a 'Direct Tax' in India?
sol: Income tax is paid directly by individuals.

subject: Environment
q: The 'Ramsar Convention' is related to the conservation of:
sol: Treaty for the conservation of wetlands.

subject: Geography
q: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
sol: Mars, due to iron oxide on its surface.

subject: Economy
q: The 'Lorenz Curve' is used to measure:
sol: Represents the distribution of income or wealth.

subject: Geography
q: Which river is known as the 'Dakshin Ganga'?
sol: Godavari is the longest river in Peninsular India.

subject: Economy
q: The 'Base Year' for calculating GDP in India is currently:
sol: Current base year used is 2011-12.

subject: Geography
q: The 'Albedo' of an object determines its:
sol: Fraction of solar energy reflected from a surface.

subject: Polity
q: Which Article allows the President to declare a 'Financial Emergency'?
sol: Art 360 deals with Financial Emergency.

subject: Geography
q: The 'Standard Meridian' of India passes through which city?
sol: 82°30' E longitude passes through Mirzapur.

subject: Economy
q: Which sector contributes the most to India's GDP?
sol: Services sector accounts for over 50%.

subject: History
q: The 'Battle of Plassey' was fought in which year?
sol: Fought between Siraj-ud-Daulah and the British.

q: Which instrument is used to measure Atmospheric Pressure?
subject: Science
sol: A barometer measures atmospheric pressure.

q: Who was the founder of the 'Haryanka Dynasty'?
subject: History
sol: Bimbisara founded the dynasty in Magadha.

q: The 'Tropic of Cancer' does not pass through which Indian state?
subject: Geography
sol: It passes through 8 states; Odisha is not one of them.

q: Which of the following is a 'Primary Pollutant'?
subject: Environment
sol: Primary pollutants are emitted directly from sources.

q: The 'First Five Year Plan' (1951-56) gave priority to:
subject: Economy
sol: Focused primarily on the agrarian sector.

q: Who wrote the book 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India'?
subject: History
sol: Naoroji explained the 'Drain of Wealth' theory.

q: The 'Atmospheric Layer' where radio waves are reflected back is:
subject: Geography
sol: Ionosphere reflects radio signals back to Earth.

q: Which Buddhist Council was held during the reign of Ashoka?
subject: History
sol: The Third Council was held at Pataliputra.

q: The 'Gini Coefficient' is a measure of:
subject: Economy
sol: Measures the distribution of income or wealth.

q: Which acid is present in 'Ant Stings'?
subject: Science
sol: Also known as Formic Acid.

q: The 'Yom Kippur War' was fought between which entities?
subject: History
sol: Fought in 1973, also known as the October War.

q: Which of the following is a 'C3 Plant'?
subject: Science
sol: Rice uses the C3 carbon fixation pathway.

q: The 'Sarkaria Commission' dealt with:
subject: Polity
sol: Reviewed the balance of power between Centre and States.

q: What is the PH value of 'Pure Water'?
subject: Science
sol: A PH of 7 is neutral.

q: Who was the first Indian woman to become President of INC?
subject: History
sol: Sarojini Naidu presided in 1925.

q: The 'Lothal' dockyard is associated with which civilization?
subject: History
sol: A major port city of the Harappan civilization.

q: Which organ in the human body is affected by 'Glaucoma'?
subject: Science
sol: It is a group of eye conditions damaging the optic nerve.

q: The 'Fundamental Duties' were added on the recommendation of:
subject: Polity
sol: Added by the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976.

q: Which city is known as the 'Silicon Valley of India'?
subject: Geography
sol: Bengaluru is the nation's leading IT exporter.
